title: Cross-Domain Vanilla-vs-SPECTRA Agent Ablation Memory

resource_records.name: Cross-domain blinded agent-ablation run
resource_records.source: Fresh vanilla and SPECTRA agents launched from scratch on three executable domains.
resource_records.access_notes: Useful for process claims and rubric structure, not as evidence that SPECTRA uniquely discovers obvious failure axes.

resource_records.name: Strict-naive vanilla follow-up
resource_records.source: Vanilla prompt only asked: Evaluate whether the model generalizes.
resource_records.access_notes: Important negative control: vanilla agents still found schema-obvious axes.

similarity_axes.name: composition_weighted_jaccard
similarity_axes.definition: Schema-derived molecular novelty axis selected by the SPECTRA agent when RDKit-based axes were not evaluable.
quality_gates: Classify RDKit-dependent axes as not evaluable if RDKit is unavailable.

similarity_axes.name: nearest_train_mutation_position_similarity
similarity_axes.definition: Use distance from held-out mutations to nearest training mutation positions as a sequence-fitness novelty axis.
quality_gates: Report when the curve is non-explanatory.
Do not force monotonicity if predictions are globally poor or constant.

similarity_axes.name: component_support_fraction
similarity_axes.definition: For combination perturbations, measure what fraction of components appeared as supported training singles.
quality_gates: Evaluate component support at the scientific-unit level.
Mark response-profile similarity as post-hoc or leaky for split design.
Report gene/cell support axes that collapse or are not evaluable.

findings.claim: Across molecules, sequence fitness, and perturbation biology, SPECTRA agents produced more complete, reproducible, leakage-aware audit artifacts than vanilla agents.
evidence: Initial blinded scores: molecules 24/24 versus 17/24, sequence fitness 23/24 versus 17/24, perturbation biology 24/24 versus 18/24.
SPECTRA produced pairwise files, split stats, performance curves, AUSPC/equivalent summaries, failed-axis reporting, leakage classification, and audit cards.
limitations: The capsules exposed schema-obvious failure axes.
This supports audit quality and standardization rather than unique discovery.

findings.claim: Strict-naive vanilla agents still identified the main generalization failures in these capsules without being prompted with similarity or novelty language.
evidence: Strict-naive scores: molecules 21/24, sequence fitness 22/24, perturbation biology 22/24.
Vanilla inferred SMILES overlap/target shift, mutation-position holdout/constant predictions, and perturbation component support.
limitations: This result means these capsules are not hard agent-discovery benchmarks.
Future discovery claims need less schema-obvious tasks or model-paper-to-external-resource investigations.

reuse_guidance: Use this entry when designing with-/without-SPECTRA agent evaluations; keep the strict-naive vanilla prompt as the main control.
Report process metrics separately from unique-discovery claims.
Do not use schema-obvious capsules as proof that vanilla agents need SPECTRA to discover the failure.
Reuse the 12-dimension rubric for audit completeness scoring.
do_not_repeat: Do not lead the vanilla agent with similarity, novelty, curve, or being-tested language.
Do not claim SPECTRA is necessary for discovery on these easy capsules.
Do not merge deterministic benchmark results with fresh-agent behavioral results without labeling the difference.

artifact_paths.label: dataset catalog entries for reproduced domains
